verdict_reason: BG 2.8 sits in cross-traditional consensus territory — Shankara, Ramanuja, and Madhva all read it as the formal closure of Arjuna's case-making before Krishna's reply at 2.11. The engineering layer does not borrow a tradition-specific reading; it stays restricted to the consensus claim that no external configuration (rājya, ādhipatya) can dispel the state, and the chapter takes this seriously as a precondition for a different kind of teaching. No load-bearing technical terms are dropped — śoka and indriya are present in the source pack and the engineering layer preserves their force in plain English ('the underlying state I am in,' 'diagnostic limit'), which is acceptable for the engineering register since neither is sectarianly contested in this verse. PASS.
